_meta.name: Curated Drug Interaction Knowledge Base (Demo)
_meta.source_note: Illustrative, offline dataset for hackathon demonstration only. NOT a substitute for clinical resources (e.g., NIH RxNav, Lexicomp, Micromedex).

interactions.mechanism: Additive inhibition of hemostasis (anticoagulant + antiplatelet) markedly increases bleeding risk.
interactions.management: Avoid combination unless specifically directed by a clinician; monitor for bleeding.

interactions.mechanism: NSAID increases bleeding risk and can displace warfarin from protein binding, raising INR.
interactions.management: Prefer acetaminophen for analgesia; avoid chronic NSAID use.

interactions.mechanism: NSAID potentiates anticoagulant effect and irritates GI mucosa, raising GI bleed risk.
interactions.management: Avoid; use alternative analgesia under guidance.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP2C9/CYP3A4, reducing warfarin metabolism and increasing INR.
interactions.management: Monitor INR closely; consider alternative antibiotic.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin strongly inhibits CYP3A4, raising simvastatin levels and risk of rhabdomyolysis.
interactions.management: Contraindicated; suspend statin or use non-interacting antibiotic.

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4; combined with simvastatin increases myopathy risk.
interactions.management: Limit simvastatin dose (max 20 mg/day) or switch statin.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4, modestly increasing atorvastatin exposure.
interactions.management: Limit atorvastatin to 20 mg/day during the course.

interactions.mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, reducing activation of clopidogrel (antiplatelet effect blunted).
interactions.management: Prefer pantoprazole if PPI needed with clopidogrel.

interactions.mechanism: Both raise serum potassium; combination risks dangerous hyperkalemia.
interactions.management: Monitor potassium and renal function; avoid if eGFR low.

interactions.mechanism: ACE inhibitor reduces potassium excretion; supplements can cause hyperkalemia.
interactions.management: Avoid routine potassium supplements; monitor levels.

interactions.mechanism: Potassium-sparing diuretic + supplement risks severe hyperkalemia (cardiac arrhythmia).
interactions.management: Do not combine without close monitoring.

interactions.mechanism: Trimethoprim has potassium-sparing effect; additive hyperkalemia with spironolactone.
interactions.management: Monitor potassium closely.

interactions.mechanism: Both lower blood pressure via different pathways; combination can cause life-threatening hypotension.
interactions.management: Absolute contraindication; never use nitrates with PDE5 inhibitors.

interactions.mechanism: SSRIs + tramadol lower seizure threshold and increase serotonergic effects.
interactions.management: Use caution; monitor for seizures/serotonin syndrome.

interactions.mechanism: Additive serotonergic activity raises serotonin syndrome and seizure risk.
interactions.management: Monitor closely; consider alternative analgesic.

interactions.mechanism: Triptan + SSRI carries small serotonin syndrome risk.
interactions.management: Use lowest effective triptan dose; counsel on symptoms.

interactions.mechanism: Serotonergic combination; rare serotonin syndrome reported.
interactions.management: Monitor; counsel patient on warning signs.

interactions.mechanism: NSAID reduces methotrexate clearance, raising toxic levels (bone marrow suppression).
interactions.management: Avoid NSAIDs, especially high-dose MTX; use acetaminophen.

interactions.mechanism: Additive folate antagonism increases marrow toxicity and pancytopenia risk.
interactions.management: Avoid combination where possible.

interactions.mechanism: Verapamil reduces digoxin clearance, risking digoxin toxicity (arrhythmia, nausea).
interactions.management: Reduce digoxin dose; monitor levels closely.

interactions.mechanism: Diltiazem modestly raises digoxin levels.
interactions.management: Monitor digoxin levels; adjust dose.

interactions.mechanism: Both depress AV node; combination risks severe bradycardia / heart block.
interactions.management: Avoid concurrent use; if needed, close cardiac monitoring.

interactions.mechanism: Chronic alcohol use alters warfarin metabolism and GI mucosa, raising bleed risk.
interactions.management: Limit alcohol; monitor INR.

interactions.mechanism: Additive blood-pressure lowering; risk of symptomatic hypotension.
interactions.management: Monitor BP; counsel on dizziness.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs blunt ACE inhibitor effect and risk renal impairment ('triple whammy' if diuretic added).
interactions.management: Limit NSAID use; monitor renal function.
